channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: How Trump backed away from promising to end the Russia-Ukraine war in 24 hours
item.description: During his campaign, Donald Trump said repeatedly that he would be able to end the war between Russia and Ukraine “in 24 hours” upon taking office.
item.dc:creator: Meg Kinnard, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Appeals court halts Boasberg’s contempt proceedings against Trump administration
item.description: A divided federal appeals court panel on Friday temporarily halted U.S. District Judge James Boasberg’s contempt proceedings against the Trump administration over its deportation flights to El Salvador last month. The U.S. Court of Appeals for the D.C.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Judge orders Trump administration to tell fired workers they were not let go for poor performance
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A U.S. district judge in San Francisco on Friday ordered the Trump administration to provide probationary workers fired en masse a written statement saying they were not terminated for performance reasons, but as part of a government-wide termination.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fired probationary workers at Commerce say their insurance is being cut early
item.description: Probationary workers who were refired this month from the Commerce Department say their health insurance is being terminated earlier than they expected.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, NewsNation

item.title: Judge declines to further enforce order restoring AP access to White House
item.description: A federal judge on Friday declined to further enforce his order directing the White House to restore The Associated Press’s access to certain spaces, signaling that while top officials are owed a presumption of good faith, violations of his directive would be met with a heavy hand. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Trump taps Mark Levin, Henry McMaster to ‘revamped’ Homeland Security council
item.description: President Trump announced Thursday he has picked Fox News contributor Mark Levin, South Carolina Gov. Henry McMaster and other allies to serve on a "revamped" Homeland Security Advisory Council.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, Fox 5

item.title: Minnesota baseball lovers make a pitch to honor bygone ballpark, 2 historic St. Paul teams
item.description: The St. Paul Saints and St. Paul Colored Gophers played ball in the early 1900s at a tiny ballpark near the Capitol called the Pillbox. The Colored Gophers were a vital piece of Black baseball history nationally. A local historian is lobbying now for a public commemoration of the site.
item.dc:creator: Peter Cox, MPR News

item.title: Tropical drinks by the pool? Not so fast, says senator who visited Abrego Garcia in El Salvador
item.description: There was the pool furniture in the background. There were the tropical drinks, which looked to be margaritas garnished with cherries. And then there were the deported prisoner and the American senator, sitting and chatting.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump moves to invoke Schedule F to make it easier to fire some federal workers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump has begun making one of the controversial personnel changes for government employees that was spelled out in the conservative Project 2025 blueprint for his second term.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Judge pauses Trump administration's plans for mass layoffs at Consumer Financial Protection Bureau
item.description: The decision leaves in limbo a bureau created after the Great Recession to safeguard against fraud, abuse and deceptive practices. Trump administration officials argue that it has overstepped its authority and should have a more limited mission.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Judge blocks Trump administration from passport changes affecting some transgender Americans
item.description: A federal judge has partially blocked the Trump administration from enacting a policy that bans the use of “X” marker used by many nonbinary people on passports as well as the changing of gender markers.
item.dc:creator: Michael Casey, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Judge says detained Tufts student must be transferred from Louisiana to Vermont
item.description: A federal judge has ordered that a Tufts University student from Turkey who was detained by immigration authorities in March be brought to Vermont.
item.dc:creator: Holly Ramer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Federal judge orders halt to Trump admin's CFPB terminations
item.description: A federal judge ordered the Trump administration to temporarily halt mass layoffs at the Consumer Financial Protections Bureau after an appeals court had previously narrowed an earlier injunction.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pentagon's week of power struggles: Leak fallout and shouting matches hit Hegseth's inner circle
item.description: Multiple defense officials describe a bitter internal feud behind the sudden ouster of three aides to Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth — with finger-pointing and accusations flying.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: California to sue Trump administration over AmeriCorps cuts
item.description: Gov. Gavin Newsom announced late Thursday that California will sue the Trump administration over the Department of Government Efficiency’s cuts to AmeriCorps.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Politics Friday: Digging into Minnesota‘s political history
item.description: MPR News politics editor Brian Bakst and a team of MPR News reporters and producers share a bit Minnesota political history with some modern twists.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: White House photo bluntly shows where parties stand on immigration amid Abrego Garcia deportation
item.description: The White House releases contrasting images showing Trump comforting grieving mother Patty Morin while Democrat senator Van Hollen meets with deported illegal immigrant in El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump calls FSU shooting a ‘shame,’ says he’ll always protect Second Amendment
item.description: President Trump on Thursday called a deadly shooting at Florida State University (FSU) "terrible," but he dismissed the possibility of it spurring any changes to gun laws in the country. "Look, I'm a big advocate of the Second Amendment. I have been from the beginning.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Oklahoma House passes 'Christ is King' resolution
item.description: Every Republican present on the floor voted for the resolution and every Democrat present voted against.
item.dc:creator: Hunter Elyse/ KFOR, NewsNation

item.title: Trump calls Sen. Van Hollen a 'fool' after meeting with deported MS-13 suspect in El Salvador
item.description: President Donald Trump is calling Sen. Chris Van Hollen a "fool" after the Maryland Democrat flew to El Salvador to meet with Kilmar Abrego Garcia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump admin to cut 90% of CFPB in latest layoffs: Reports
item.description: President Donald Trump’s administration is cutting nearly 90 percent of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau’s (CFPB) employees in a push to significantly downsize the watchdog agency formed to shield U.S. consumers from financial fraud and abuse.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Surprise open governor's seat in Iowa signals competitive GOP primary, glimmer of hope for Democrats
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — Iowa looked a lot different in 2010, when Kim Reynolds was first elected to statewide office. Reynolds was the Republican nominee for lieutenant governor on the ticket alongside former Gov. Terry Branstad.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Law firms, universities and now civil society groups are in Trump's sights for punitive action
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — First the nation’s top law firms. Then its premier universities. Now, President Donald Trump is leaning on the advocacy groups that underpin U.S. civil society.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rubio and Witkoff met Ukrainian and European officials for landmark talks on Ukraine's future
item.description: PARIS (AP) — France hosted high-level talks Thursday to discuss Ukraine and its security, the first time since President Donald Trump's inauguration that top American, Ukrainian and European officials are known to have met together to discuss an end to the war. U.S.
item.dc:creator: EMMA BURROWS and ANGELA CHARLTON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nearly 90% of Consumer Financial Protection Bureau cut as Trump's government downsizing continues
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is drastically shrinking the workforce and mission of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau, eviscerating an agency created after the Great Recession with the goal of protecting Americans from fraud, abuse and deceptive practices.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US to withdraw 600 troops from Syria, leaving fewer than 1,000 to help counter IS militants
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. will withdraw about 600 troops from Syria, leaving fewer than 1,000 to work with Kurdish allies to counter the Islamic State group, a U.S. official said Thursday. The official spoke on the condition of anonymity to discuss details not yet announced publicly. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge grants detained U of M student bond but he‘ll stay in ICE custody as feds appeal
item.description: An international student facing deportation was granted bond by a judge earlier this week. However, he will stay locked up after the Department of Homeland Security filed an appeal.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Thamer, MPR News

item.title: Noem threatens Harvard with ban on foreign student visas
item.description: Noem sent a “scathing letter” to Harvard demanding the university hand over records of foreign students.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court to hear rare May arguments in birthright citizenship battle
item.description: The Supreme Court scheduled a rare May oral argument on the Trump administration’s emergency request to narrow a series of nationwide blocks on President Trump’s executive order that would restrict birthright citizenship.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Barack Obama, Bernie Sanders, Ben Carson among most liked politicians, surveys show
item.description: In one YouGov survey on Democratic figures, 58 percent of respondents said they felt “very favorable” or “somewhat favorable” toward Obama.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Former DeSantis spokesperson accused of exposing himself at mall
item.description: A New College of Florida official and former spokesperson for Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis was arrested Tuesday and charged with exposure of sexual organs.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, NewsNation

item.title: Florida House votes to stop pickleball courts, hotels from being built in state parks
item.description: A bill aiming to protect Florida's state parks from overdevelopment was unanimously passed Wednesday by the state House.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Wisconsin squeaks past Minnesota for the top spot in voter turnout
item.description: The Minnesota Secretary of State confirms Wisconsin narrowly surpassed Minnesota with voter turnout in the 2024 election.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters and Peter Cox, MPR News

item.title: Lawmakers press Hope Florida officials over political contributions
item.description: Lawmakers questioned Hope Florida officials Tuesday morning to further investigate those involved in the ongoing probe into the non-profit and a mysterious money trail tied to a $10 million Medicaid settlement.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump administration freezes $2.2 billion in grants to Harvard over campus activism
item.description: The federal government says it’s freezing more than $2.2 billion in grants and $60 million in contracts to Harvard University, since the institution said Monday it won’t comply with the Trump administration’s demands to limit activism on campus.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News
